title: IDF: Palestinian Groups Violated Gaza Ceasefire 139 Times Since October 2025
detail: On April 6, 2026, the IDF released figures showing Palestinian militant groups had violated the Gaza ceasefire a total of 139 times since the truce took effect in October 2025. Of these, 22 violations occurred between February 28 and April 6, 2026 — the period overlapping with the Israel-Iran escalation. Violations catalogued include mortar fire, IED placements near IDF observation posts, and armed militants approaching the buffer zone. The IDF stated it responded with targeted strikes in each case. The report comes amid broader regional instability due to the Iran-Israel conflict and growing concerns about whether the ceasefire framework can hold. The Board of Peace framework and Phase 2 negotiations remain frozen as Hamas awaits the outcome of the Iran-Israel confrontation before committing to disarmament talks.

sources.name: Foundation for Defense of Democracies (FDD) — citing IDF

media.caption: IDF reports 139 total ceasefire violations since October 2025 ceasefire began

title: IDF Targets Hamas Weapons Smuggler Ali Ahmed Ali Amrain in Central Gaza
detail: On April 6, 2026, the IDF reported striking Hamas operative Ali Ahmed Ali Amrain in the central Gaza Strip, describing him as a key figure involved in weapons smuggling into Gaza in violation of ceasefire terms. The IDF stated Amrain was responsible for coordinating the transfer of arms through tunnel networks and had been under surveillance for several weeks. The strike was one of several targeted operations conducted during the ceasefire period against individuals the IDF characterizes as posing imminent threats. Palestinian health officials in Gaza confirmed the strike resulted in casualties. The operation reflects Israel's continued position that targeted counterterrorism actions are permitted under ceasefire terms, a stance Hamas contests.

sources.name: Foundation for Defense of Democracies (FDD) — citing IDF

media.caption: IDF targeted Hamas weapons smuggler in central Gaza during ceasefire
media.source: Foundation for Defense of Democracies / IDF
